channel.title: nlin.SI updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: nlin.SI updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: On the Darboux-Halphen system: Jacobi vs Lie
item.description: arXiv:2605.13463v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Two constructions of the Darboux-Halphen system are discussed. In the Jacobi construction we start with multi-valued functions which are fixed as the first integrals. In the Lie construction we use single-valued representation of the simple algebra which is non-integrable in Lie's terminology.
item.dc:creator: A. V. Tsiganov

item.title: Collapsing in polygonal dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2507.16432v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We define polygonal dynamics as a family of dynamical systems acting on points in projective spaces. The most famous example is the pentagram map. Similar collapsing phenomena seem to occur in most of these systems. We prove it in some case, and conjecture that it almost always happens. Moreover, we give a formula for the limit point in term of roots of $d+1$ degree polynomials (where $d$ is the dimension of the projective space). We do so by generalizing Glick's operator, interpreted as an infinitesimal monodromy. This answers questions about its reappearance in many systems, together with preserved quantities. We apply these results to several polygonal dynamics in $\mathbb{P}^1$ and introduce a new one called ``staircase'' cross-ratio dynamics, for which we study particular configurations.
item.dc:creator: Jean-Baptiste Stiegler
